title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Program Brief: Evidence Ladder
summary: A studio says “make Asteroids” but has not defined the player, verbs, success, failure, control needs, sensory alternatives, supported devices, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the evidence ladder increment in player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter. Learners progress through predict…
objectives: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.
Record input, vision, hearing, motion, cognition, timing, text, difficulty, and assistive-technology needs before implementation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Predict Evidence Ladder · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3807 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 3807 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. A defensible solution applies Evidence Ladder, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-evidence-ladder — Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-4
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-outcomes-repo-accessibility-4.
# Case 1655: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death.
def worked_asteroids_outcomes_repo_accessibility_4(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "playerworked4",
        "seed1655",
        "input4dekrow",
        "frameworked41655",
        "state1655worked4",
        "playtest4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Evidence Ladder evidence · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-bka3p1" that uses case 8785 to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Evidence Ladder evidence · Theory Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Evidence Ladder into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Build Evidence Ladder evidence · Theory Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-evidence-ladder-bka3p1
# Competency: asteroids-evidence-ladder.
# Case 8785: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_evidence_ladder_bka3p1(player_goal: str = "survive-and-score", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, playtest_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_bka3p1 = "bka3p1-1p3akb-8785"
    assert evidence_variant_bka3p1
    evidence_axes_bka3p1 = (
        "playerbka3p1",
        "seed8785",
        "input1p3akb",
        "framebka3p18785",
        "state8785bka3p1",
        "playtest1p3akb8785",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_bka3p1)) == 6
    if not player_goal.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-game"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not playtest_owner:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "unowned-evidence"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-game-charter"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 7841 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.
